PROCALCITOKIN | 0.93 ng/ml 0.0-0.50
Comments I .
oO Procalcitonin (PCT) is a prohormone expressod by neuroendocrine cells (C cells of thyroid,
pulmenary and pancreatic tissues) present in very low levels in healthy individuals.
Measurement of PCT can be used as a marker of severe sepsis and generally grades well
with the degree of sepsis. Evidence is emerging that PCT levels can reduce unnecessary .
antibiotic prescribing in ower respiratory tract infections.
Increased Levels |
Infectious - Bacterial infections leading to Septic shock / Severe sepsis, Community
acquired respiretory tract infections & Ventilatog kuced prcumonia .
Non Infectious = Severe Cardiogenic shock, organ perfusion enomalics, Small cell hing
cancet or Medullary C-cell carcinoms of thyroid, major trauma, surgical intervention &
severe bums, treatment which stimulate the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines &
Neonates (<48 hrs. afler birth)
As a prognostic marker to support outcome prediction in sepsis patients.
As an indicatoe of severity and major complications in acute pancreatitis.
Asa guide for the necessity of antibiotic therapy and to monitor success of treatments in
